question: Is the gallery room (walls, floor, ceiling lights, pedestal structure) preserved from the first image without significant unintended changes?
Is the low-poly dinosaur placed on top of the central white pedestal?
Does the dinosaur sculpture retain the distinctive low-poly, faceted geometry of the input model?
Does the dinosaur's material appear to be matte spray-painted metal rather than the original digital/paper appearance?
Are there subtle visual details indicating physical fabrication (e.g., seams or slight wear) on the dinosaur sculpture?
Does the dinosaur exhibit clear highlight and shadow planes consistent with the overhead spotlights?
Does the dinosaur cast a visible shadow on the pedestal surface?
Does the dinosaur cast a shadow onto the floor that is consistent with the overhead lighting?
Is the ink portrait displayed as a framed artwork on the left wall?
Is the subject of the framed artwork recognizable as the elderly man from the input sketch?
Does the artwork within the frame retain the ink line drawing style of the input portrait?
Does the portrait feature a frame with visible thickness or depth?
Is the framed portrait perspective-distorted to align correctly with the receding angle of the left wall?
Are there visible reflections on the portrait surface suggesting the presence of glass?
Do the glass reflections on the portrait plausibly reflect the gallery's overhead light strips?
Are the dinosaur and framed portrait scaled appropriately for the pedestal and wall respectively?
Are the edges of the added objects (dinosaur and frame) clean and free from obvious compositing halos?
Is the dinosaur free from severe geometric distortions or malformed limbs?
